text: The surgeon performs lateral dissection of the colon, aiming to improve visualization of the entire colon for potential external features of cancer. A colonoscope is brought into the room for an on-table colonoscopy to locate the lesion.

The surgeon performs lateral mobilization of the colon, revealing severe diverticulosis throughout the bowel. The visual inspection shows the disease extending up to the splenic flexure, indicating the need for a full left hemicolectomy.

The surgeon performs an intraoperative colonoscopy, navigating through the colon with visible diverticula and large stool balls intermittently obstructing the lumen. The colonoscope is advanced up to the splenic flexure, approximately seventy centimeters from the anal verge, with careful withdrawal and camera adjustments to provide an endoscopic view of the colon's interior.
